behavior_summary: Provides the response when the player examines the FISHNET object: it displays a short descriptive text 'fishing net' and then yields/ends the U8P_FISHNET::look process.
No branching, menu interaction, external calls, or global flag manipulation are present; the routine is purely cosmetic and local to the object instance.
The function runs as a process-type U8P_FISHNET::look handler (processType 0x0208) and terminates via a suspend/exit instruction after emitting the bark.
notable_control_flow: U8P_FISHNET::look emits a single Bark ('fishing net') and immediately follows with a suspend/terminate, indicating the U8P_FISHNET::look process ends after description.
There are no conditional branches, loops, or calls inside the U8P_FISHNET::look process — straight-line behavior.
open_questions: Is the displayed string 'fishing net' a hardcoded token or a localization key resolved elsewhere?
Are there variant U8P_FISHNET::look texts for different contexts (e.g., when the item is owned or in-use) that are implemented outside this extracted fragment?
Is this object's U8P_FISHNET::look handler ever extended or overridden by higher-level scripts or prototypes at runtime?
